same_tag_doctrine_consistency.architectural-invariant: Consistent with 2.11 and 2.12. The chain so far: 2.11 (don't grieve implementation while citing first-principles), 2.12 (the substrate is eternal — predates and outlasts), 2.13 (the substrate is preserved across analogous state transitions). All three verses teach the same doctrine, building on each other.
same_tag_doctrine_consistency.spec-vs-behavior: Consistent. The verse adds the conformance-suite predicate: spec is what the suite encodes, behavior is what the implementation does on a given day.
same_tag_doctrine_consistency.interface-stability: First use of this tag in the chapter; correctly applied — the contract is the interface, and its stability is what is being claimed.

cross_reference_check: No cross-references declared in this verse — n/a.
verdict_reason: Tags consistent with 2.11 and 2.12. The sāṅkhya block A teaching is coherent: the substrate is real, eternal, and preserved across transitions; the operator who has located it is steady. PASS.
